ogTitle: Wards Mistake vs Wareemba: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wards Mistake and Wareemba.
heading: Wards Mistake vs Wareemba — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wards Mistake, New South Wales and Wareemba, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wards Mistake has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Wareemba (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wards Mistake or Wareemba safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wards Mistake has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Wareemba scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wards Mistake scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Wareemba scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wards Mistake or Wareemba?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wards Mistake has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
